review_title: Unified Secure Directory
description: Overall, JumpCloud delivers a powerful and unified directory experience with strong security and multi-platform support. However, the learning curve and occasional UI complexity can make onboarding slightly challenging for new administrators.
author: Saravana P.
pros: I like that JumpCloud Directory centralizes user and device management in one place, making administration very efficient. Its zero-trust security model and broad integration support make it both secure and highly flexible for any environment.
cons: The interface can feel complex at times, especially for new users managing many policies and integrations.

review_title: Managing Directory and MDM Simply
description: Working with users, user-groups, SAML, OIDC, MSM, integrations with API (including PowerShell and Python). Customizing policies, attaching to endpoints, really easy management.
author: Tomer C.
pros: SaaS, intuitive UX, great support, easy API capabilities with both PowerShell and Python, push notification for MFA, documentation is great. MDM feature is really good too, the agents on the laptops are good and not consuming much resources.
cons: It took a while to implement OIDC instead of SAML apps. Sometimes a little bit behind the market, but what is being released is really good (not many bugs).

review_title: Really good and still improving
description: Easy to work with, good integration with different system flavours. Easy to deploy and implement companywide in short time.
author: Lucas L.
pros: Way better than Entra ID for large organisations using windows, linux and mac devices. Everything in one place, easy integration.
cons: Not everything is available (at least for windows) like in Active Directory, but most common settings are there for sure.

review_title: JC feedback
author: Ori T.
pros: straightforward, easy to use. Deploys fast and gets policies immediately overall happy, but not my first choice
cons: unable to host its own pkg/msi no environment variables in mobileconfig, e.g: {username}, {email} no DDM support for Apple
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great platform !
description: Im in my learning curve and obtaining training and request permision rights to access to the core functionality in the admin platform.
author: Oscar D.
pros: The user interface is very simple and visually appealing. It facilitates teamwork and we believe it offers good value for money.
cons: I'm still not entirely comfortable with the platform and I'm finding it a bit difficult to mentally switch from Intune to JumpCloud.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: JumpCloud Should Intergrate better with Microsoft
description: I chose JumpCloud so I wouldn't have to rely on Microsoft so much (Azure AD in particular), and in many ways, JC has allowed me to do this especially in the areas like cloud directories, identity and SSO. However, it's lack of native integration with MS/Azure in many ways prevents me from achieving levels of security I would like to achieve. Because of this, I have at times considered migrating from JC to Azure, Intune, and other mS products, but I have not - yet - in the hopes JC can solve these problems.
author: Mac C.
pros: Ease of use. Cross domain capabilities Identity and Cloud Directory integrations Dynamic groups (user and device)
cons: The most frustrating gap in JumpCloud MDM is no native Microsoft Partner Compliance Management (PCM) integration so I can apply more restrictive controls around M365 apps from non managed devices. Software Management is too limited. It is common to have to uninstall and reinstall an application on a single device, but group assignments make this difficult. When I remove JC's management of an application it doesn't uninstall the application leaving it "orphaned" in a sense.

review_title: System acceptable, though even advanced tiers lack functionality that they will sell as add-ons
description: It's fine, though I wouldn't say I'm tied to it. It's a system like others, lots of add ons, generally does its job and has become more reliable in recent years.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It simplifies the management of both users and devices. I can provision users across my various systems and devices as well quickly adjust permissions as well as groups with the click of a button
cons: Costs are rising with minimal feature enhancements. Our costs are now nearly double what they were 5 years ago, but the platform itself is largely the same. The extra features being added are mostly add-ons, not included.

review_title: A Great All-in-One Solution for Our IT Needs
description: Overall, my experience with JumpCloud has been really positive. Once everything was set up, the platform made managing users, devices, and access a lot simpler and more organized. I like that it works smoothly across different operating systems and that most routine tasks—like onboarding new employees or enforcing security policies—can be handled quickly without jumping through a lot of hoops. There were a few moments early on where I had to spend some time figuring out where certain settings lived, but once I got used to the layout, everything felt pretty straightforward. The platform has been reliable, and it’s taken a lot of manual work off my plate. In short, JumpCloud has made our identity and device management more efficient, more secure, and generally less stressful.
author: Dipak P.
pros: What I like most about JumpCloud is that it just makes my day-to-day work easier. I don’t have to jump between a bunch of different tools anymore—user management, devices, and access controls are all in one place. The setup was pretty straightforward, and once everything was running, it honestly felt like the system kind of “got out of the way” and just worked. I also really appreciate that it handles different operating systems without any drama. Our team uses a mix of Windows and Macs, and JumpCloud manages both without me having to babysit anything. The security features, like MFA and policies, are simple to roll out and don’t confuse users, which is a huge plus. Overall, it’s reliable, easy to use, and saves me a lot of time.
cons: The main downside for me is that some of the more advanced settings aren’t always obvious at first—you sometimes have to click around a bit or dig into the documentation to figure out where certain options are. It’s not a deal-breaker, but there’s a bit of a learning curve if you want to use the platform to its full potential. I’ve also noticed that a few updates roll out without much explanation, so occasionally things look different and I have to re-orient myself. And while support is generally good, response times can vary depending on the issue. Overall, the platform works well, but a little more clarity and consistency in the UI and documentation would make it even better.

review_title: Streamlining IT with Simplicity and Security
description: My overall experience with JumpCloud has been very positive. The platform offers a seamless way to manage users, devices, and access across an organization, saving time and reducing complexity. Its intuitive interface, reliable performance, and strong security features make day-to-day management much easier. While there are areas for improvement, such as expanded Linux support, the platform consistently delivers value and flexibility, and it has become an essential tool in our IT workflow.
author: shake M.
pros: I appreciate most about JumpCloud is the way it unifies identity, device, and access management into one platform. Being able to manage users, enforce security policies, handle authentication, and oversee both Windows/macOS devices from a single console has dramatically simplified daily operations. The platform’s zero-touch onboarding, browser-based management, and seamless SSO integrations save a significant amount of time. I also really value the balance JumpCloud strikes between ease of use and robust security controls — it’s powerful without being complicated.
cons: While JumpCloud is an excellent platform overall, one area for improvement is broader Linux support. Currently, certain versions like Fedora 42 and 43 aren’t fully supported, which can be limiting for organizations using those distributions. Expanding compatibility with more Linux versions would make the platform even more versatile and valuable for diverse environments.

review_title: Jumpcloud knows what you need for I.T. in a Mac+Windows+AlltheSaaS'es world.
description: I've been using the service for over 4 years. It has steadily exceeded expectations by adding features in areas that IT actually needs help with. I would hate to lose it and rebuild our management toolset with a scattered list of Intune,365,Jamf, ...excel!
author: Ken F.
pros: It integrates the most import functions in one place and it does it well. IAM, MDM, Resources. A single pane of glass that allows you to keep up with the needs of your organization.
cons: In rare cases you need to do some maintenance of agents. It's the same with any service but we all want that number to be zero.

review_title: One of the best standalone directory service out there
author: Farooq A.
pros: It's easy to use and navigate. Stores most of the information in appropriate attributes. Has all the capabilities like password management, SSO, Radius, etc.
cons: I think everything is good, so I don't have any dislikes. Licensing can be more competitive, I guess. They do have room to grow into a giant like Entra ID. So, all the best for that.

review_title: Great functional product that tackles most of the Central management and directory functions
description: Jumpclouds offering has great features and helps simplify the central user management issue we had. Importing users from Gsuite which was the main system that housed all our users centrally was simple, albeit lacking granular import options (such as ignore mailgroups etc) Systems (which are devices) is the inventory of all your systems running the jumpcloud agent - the greatest feature here is being able to set MFA for logging into your mac, which is very useful if you'd like to bring that security practice in. Actioning user changes is pretty quick and straightforward but there are some caveats on some of the more everyday features that could be laid out in a slicker fashion. For example resetting a users password cannot be done from a click of a button - you have to go into the account and manually set a new one, or invite the user to reset it themselves, but if you've integrated JC and Gsuite, and they cant get into their email, which partly breaks our self serve functionality we're tyring to bring to the company. The idea of being able to deploy and manage the admin account when a mac is managed via the agent is a helpful addition taking away the need to implement one through various other means. Policies as they stand are a little different to what your typical "mdm polices" are. So theres a bit of a distinction to pick up with that. The Agent & account tis a less forgiving implementation and requires that the account names on the macs match the JC directory.
author: Robert R.
pros: Support right through the demo to the purchase, to the implementation. Success criteria are agreed between yourselves and them. They're improving the product almost daily and have the ability to use web-hooks to monitor service status' The ease in which you are able to integrate Gsuite (as well as multiple ways to import users from your existing central directory) It also gives you the ability to tailor the input for mass imports to limit the need to get users involved in this stage. The ability to simply submit Ideas to improve the UI. The communication from their design team and being involved in user testing so early on. The resource and Knowledge base is extensive and easy to read - one of the best Ive used from a company in this space. The interface is also simple to use and navigate through without being buried under complex sub menus Straightforward email notifications from the system. Being able to simply reimport users youve deleted without having to wait for a sync or overlapping the account when reimporting.
cons: A few tricky bits around JC > Gsuite integration wording that take some time to get your head around when integrating and activating accounts once imported. The admin vs user console login is dynamic and handled via one URL making it a little frustrating to see what the user profile looks like as you have to log out/open a new window to log in as a user - they should be separate logins URLS/Pages or have dropdowns within the admin page in order to switch between. The lack of global settings to apply around Security means for both user login and System security - you have to apply MFA individually to users When removing/disabling users there isnt an ability to change what happens to their email account - they will be disabled/suspended immediately. Not able to tailor the automated emails that come from the system to your users as it stands Importing users via OUs inside Gsuite isnt possible (its everyone or needing to know user by user who to import (not the end of the world but worthwhile noting if you have more than 200 users + copious amounts of group emails) Currently no ability to assign users to OUs from within JC to Gsuite. We utilitise divisions and Departments as sub groups for organising our employees and use this hierarchy in another solution. Youre currently not able to nest groups so we had to flat out create ever department as top level inside the "system of users" section. Again a little niche for us, and shouldnt mark this awesome product down
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Look out Microsoft AD...
description: I was hired into my organization to bring on a directory platform and consolidate our IT practices among users and computers. I came from a microsoft active directory shop, but my new company was not interested in AD. That was a seemingly tall task, until I found JumpCloud. I thoroughly enjoy coming into work and using jumpcloud to build the next era of our IT practices and procedures. We are primarily focused on SSO at the moment, and that is proving to be very simple to configure. I am looking forward to getting devices managed with JC in the next coming months using their pre-defined policies, as well as their command runner feature and powershell module. JumpCloud will soon be a serious competitor to Microsoft's admin suite, and I am very happy to be on board.
author: Trevor M.
pros: SSO integrations are incredibly easy to configure, no matter if you are using a prebuilt configuration or need to make a custom connector. Having SSO, LDAP, Radius, MFA in the same platform as device authentication and configuration policies is something I have not found outside of Microsoft AD and JumpCloud. There are remote management and monitoring tools out there that claim to do these things, but so far JumpCloud has proven to be incredibly simple. I am constantly amazed that my tests just work within the first few tries. I am used to Microsoft environments where testing gets very tedious and gives minimal feedback on how to make things work. JumpCloud supports MacBooks like a champion. If your office is heavily invested in MacBooks, and you are looking for an easy way to centrally manage authentication and hardening, JumpCloud is the tool for you. By far my favorite feature is "Submit an idea" right in the admin console. You get the sense that they really care about their admins and want to make a tool that is always getting better.
cons: 1) No nested user groups! I feel this is essential for identity and access governance. 2) The device policies that they have available cover a lot of the base security hardening needs, but I am excited to see just how much of the various supported OS jumpcloud will eventually be able to tap into. I would love to see JC mirror Active Directory Group Policies as much as possible, and continue to get more configurations added for MacOS. 3) iOS and Android devices are not able to be managed by JumpCloud, but I have been told this is coming by the end of 2021/early 2022. 4) Only TOTP MFA is supported for the admin console, and after getting SSO set up on a lot of tools the pain of that is getting to me, but I know they are working on this feature as well. 5) I personally believe that a remote desktop tool would make JumpCloud the king of IT tools.

review_title: A Reliable Directory Platform That Simplifies IT Management
description: Overall, my experience with JumpCloud has been positive. It’s a solid and reliable platform that helps keep users, devices, and access organized. While there are occasional issues, it still makes day-to-day IT management much smoother.
author: Ian Faulo R.
pros: I like how JumpCloud centralizes user and device management in one place. It makes managing access much easier, especially in a mixed OS and remote setup. The integrations with other tools are also very helpful and save a lot of time.
cons: There are times when the agent runs into sync or connectivity issues, which can be a bit frustrating and usually requires extra troubleshooting. Some features also take time to fully understand, especially for new users.

review_title: Good Cost-Effective Product for mid-sized SaaS companies
author: Archit M.
pros: I really liked how unified the platform is. JumpCloud brings user management, device management, and authentication into a single portal which makes administration far more efficient and reduces double efforts.
cons: The device management features are improving, but not yet as robust as dedicated MDM tools. Occasionally, changes took a little while to propagate across users or devices, which made real-time troubleshooting harder.

review_title: Jumpcloud Platform for the Central Cloud Device and User Managment
description: Really great solution software application for the cloud user and device managment and really likes about it.
author: Bibek M.
pros: Easy to Navigate UI, clean UI to use for begineers. and Fast and complete solution for the device and user management in one platform.
cons: The new features of asset management, and need of more software applicatoin in the software management for the devices. also the pricing abit high per user.

review_title: Couldn't live without JumpCloud
description: We have used it for almost 7 years and it has worked perfectly for us. Couldn't run our business security with out.
author: Adam B.
pros: The group control for Windows and Mac is perfect for an SME. The ability to set the same permissions and setting centrally makes IT control perfect.
cons: That there isn't as many alerts as they could be. If there were more alerts if local admin accounts are created, or permissions changed.

review_title: Centralised Management for All Type of Devices & OS Support
description: Jumpcloud supports All OS Management in a Single Portal SSO Conditional Access Access Request Patch Management
author: Gopikrishnan A.
pros: Customer Support Remote Access SSO
cons: Feature Requests getting delayed support Improve Downtime

review_title: Unified Management, Ease of Use & Deployment, Cross-Platform Support
description: JumpCloud Directory Platform is generally well-regarded as a robust, unified, cloud-native solution that replaces traditional on-premises directory services like Active Directory, particularly for small to medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) with mixed-OS environments
author: Ashish D.
pros: About the entire device life cycle from onboarding to off boarding with the help of Directory Platform.
cons: Overall user experience with the JumpCloud Directory Platform is generally positive, with users praising its ability to serve as a unified, cloud-based solution for identity, access, and device management across diverse operating systems (Windows, macOS, Linux).
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to use
description: Over all it is a great product that provides a much needed use. Simple to setup and easy to use over time.
author: Jon M.
pros: Very simple to set up when you have multiple devices across the network with easy to use process that makes the setup simple.
cons: Depending on amount of system can get a little confusing when you have multiple directories and user groups.

review_title: Simple, yet effective
author: Josh S.
pros: Easy way to manage users on our guest network. Also provides great centralization for updating software on workstations
cons: Updating machines can prove to be a bit difficult at times with the agent

review_title: JumpCloud has made it a breeze to manage user accounts and access permissions for our team.
author: Laura T.
pros: The interface is user-friendly and the ability to manage multiple systems and applications from one platform is a game changer.
cons: The onboarding process could be simplified, and it would be helpful to have more integrations with third-party apps.

review_title: the best cloud platform for secure storage.
description: Although there was no cost-effective for large number of users or additional features required, JumpCloud is a cost-effective platform for small or medium business. Also, it has a user-frendly interface.
author: Nara J.
pros: JumpCloud has comprehensive security feature and cross-platform compatibility.
cons: JumpCloud can be cost-effecCtive for small organizations, whereas the costs can increase significantly as the number of users grows or as additional features are required.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Excellent tool for the Directory and MDM
description: Overall, MDM Automate Device Enrollment, robust authentication features, and directory interfaces work together to produce a great product for an organization's software management requirements. Using these services, businesses may increase productivity, significantly reduce errors, and improve device security.
author: Santhosh K.
pros: -Duo Security, which adds an extra layer of security to the login process with multi-factor authentication (MFA).-WebAuthn is another authentication method that uses public key cryptography to verify users' identities.-TOTP is a type of two-factor authentication (2FA) that uses a time-based code that changes every 30 seconds to authenticate users.- MDM Automate Device Enrollment integrates with Cloud Directory and Active Directory for remote access, making it simple to manage user access and permissions. These integrations also make it simple to add or remove users from the system and provision new devices.- MDM Automate Device Enrollment is a fantastic tool for managing software within a company. Administrators can remotely deploy and manage software updates, Also it provides insights into device usage, enhanced.
cons: - the potential of inefficiency and errors induced by having to push the command each time a selection is made-Pushing the command on a routine basis raises the risk of error. A user, for example, may select the inaccurate machine or screw up to select a device, resulting in incomplete updates or installations. As a result, devices may become vulnerable to security threats or stop working correctly.

review_title: Avoiding the mistakes of others...
description: I watched our sister company who was all Mac-based turn to the dark-side and try to take on PC client machines along with active directory...it's been a slow churn for them. No unified passwords, a home built radius server held together by a band-aid and Mac clients that see no benefit and 2 AD servers that no one truly understands...it's like some mystical dark art. We on the other hand, spun up 10 free users on Jumpcloud and never looked back, we have Linux/Mac and PC machines...as well as virtual machines all running Jumpcloud. We have Single Sign On between VPN, Wifi, NAS, System Accounts, Email... and it's all controlled from the cloud. They will never admit their mistake and come over to Jumpcloud, but I did learn by watching their slow, fragmented process for user/device management. Glad I went with JC!
author: ADam B.
pros: I love that this DaaS product "just works" right out of the box. There is plenty of good documentation to read if you are cautious like me. I found that many features just worked and were intuitive to implement. We went from a small company that was ravaged by "password fatigue" - we separate passwords for everything with no central password app. It was a nightmare. After deploying Jumpcloud, we were able to make use of many of the features hidden in our networking gear. Within a month, we had unique (per user) credentials to get into everything. VPN, Wifi, NAS, System Accounts, Email...SSO. The password complexity is adjustable and can rotate. 2FA is simple to deploy and is icing on the cake. All of this in a Mac, Linux and PC environment....you really can't ask Active Directory to do that.
cons: While the support for features and processes pertaining to Jumpcloud are documented well. At times the instructions can seem vague and maybe presume too much. However, support personnel are just an email away...they will help you "stick to the script", they are not however always well-versed in real scenarios you may have on your job-site. Nonetheless, I'll give them a C+ for walking through a couple nerve-racking installs the first time through.
